what_it_does: Defines a systemd service unit that launches the Company bridge HTTP API on port 8770 by running a Python virtualenv interpreter from /home/tim/company/.venv/bin/python with runtime/bridge.py 8770.
observations: ExecStart uses a hardcoded .venv path and passes port 8770 as an argument
Logs stdout/stderr to the journal with SyslogIdentifier=company-bridge
Grouped into company.target via PartOf and WantedBy
standards: Hardcoded /home/tim/company path and port 8770 in ExecStart
Tight coupling to company.target for suite-level lifecycle management
conventions: Follows standard systemd unit file sections [Unit]/[Service]/[Install]
Uses Type=simple for a long-running process
Uses PartOf/WantedBy to join a target
Sends output to journal and sets a syslog identifier
concerns: Absolute home directory path /home/tim/company is hardcoded
Port 8770 is hardcoded on the command line
No User= or Group= directive is specified
No EnvironmentFile or explicit environment variables
Assumes the .venv and runtime/bridge.py exist at those exact paths
notes: This is the systemd glue that keeps the Company bridge API running under the company suite target.
questions: Is this intended as a system service or a user service for user tim?
Should the service run as a specific User=tim?
What arguments or environment does runtime/bridge.py require beyond the port?
Are there other services also wired into company.target?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: the description says it is the UI face of the Suite on HTTP API :8770, and ExecStart runs the bridge on port 8770.
intention_signals: It is trying to make the bridge a managed, auto-restarting member of a larger company suite that starts after networking is available.
contribution: Records the exact command, working directory, restart behavior, logging destination, and target membership required to run the bridge as a systemd service.
summary_for_embedding: A systemd service unit that runs /home/tim/company/.venv/bin/python runtime/bridge.py 8770 from /home/tim/company, restarts on failure, logs to journal, and belongs to company.target.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a managed, auto-restarting systemd service for the Company bridge HTTP API as part of the company suite target.
